title: Friday Sermon 20 September 2002
description:
O ye who believe!  Be mindful of Allah and have firm faith in His Messenger for He will give you a double portion of His mercy and will provide for you a light wherein you will walk and will grant you forgiveness – For verily Allah is Most Forgiving, Ever Merciful.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Hadid [Iron]: 29).

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) enjoined that one who attains old age in the cause of Allah would have their dotage transformed into light on the Day of Judgment:

Narrated Amr bin Abasah: The Messenger of Allah said ‘Whoever develops some grey hair in the cause of Allah, it shall be a light for him in the Day of Judgement.'

(Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi.  'Jami al-Tirmidhi').

On the subject of the Divine light the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) said that when one adopts righteousness one obtains distinction from the others and this difference is light, in that one is granted Divine light so much so that one walks in it.  This light extends to their wisdom and their speech, it illuminates their path, and the course-ways of their senses become profusely luminous.  It is as if the Divine beneficence enters their bloodstream:

That is, O ye who believe, if you remain steadfast in righteousness and hold fast to it for the sake of Allah, with firmness and perseverance, He will single you out from among your detractors with marked distinction.  That is, you shall be blessed with a light which will accompany you wherever you go. 

That is to say, that that light would illumine all their actions and words and faculties and senses.  Their intellect would be illumined and there would be light in all that they utter.  There would be light in their eyes and in their ears and in their tongues and in their speech and in all their movements.  The ways along which they walk would be lit up.  All their ways, the ways of their faculties and their senses will be filled with light, and they will walk altogether in light.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Aina e kamalat e Islam'.  Riyaz e Hind, 1893.  'Ruhani Khazain: v'. 177, 178.  'The Essence of Islam: ii'.  349).

In death those people would be exalted who have acquired this Living Light in this very life, having brought about a kind of death on themselves in this earthly life:

In the hereafter, only that person will achieve perfect salvation, true prosperity and real happiness who has gained in this life that living and true light which turns a person, together with all his faculties and capacities and designs, towards God Almighty and whereby his lower life dies altogether, and his soul undergoes a righteous change.  What is that living and true light?  It is the Divinely bestowed capacity, which is designated certainty or perfect comprehension.  This is the power that pulls a person with its strong hand out of a dark and fearful pit and places him against a bright and peaceful background. 
 
Before this light is acquired, all righteous actions are by way of habit, and a person is likely to stumble when confronted with the least trial.

(Ahmad.  'Ayyamus Sulha.  Ziya al-Islam, 1899.  'R. K: xiv'.  245.  'Essence: iii'.  53).

Some Persian verses by the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) depict the light of the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) with reference to the profound love the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) had for the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) and the ways in which this blessed and holy luminosity affected and influenced his heart.

Some of the revelations of the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) also pertain to the Divine Light or Noor.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020920-UR.pdf
